example_sentences: Deﬁnition 21.2 Given a network and a designated vertex s , a shortest-paths tree (SPT) for s is a subnetwork containing s and all the vertices reachable from s that forms a directed tree rooted at s such that every tree path is a shortest path in the network.

By contrast, the tree path goes in the wrong direction in a digraph: There is a directed path from a vertex in a digraph to an ancestor only if there is a back edge from a descendant to that or a more distant ancestor.

example_sentences: Deﬁnition 21.2 Given a network and a designated vertex s , a shortest-paths tree (SPT) for s is a subnetwork containing s and all the vertices reachable from s that forms a directed tree rooted at s such that every tree path is a shortest path in the network.
